hook.text: One family produced eight prominent mathematicians across three generations.

buildup.text: Jakob and Johann Bernoulli were brothers who advanced calculus, probability, and physics.

discovery.text: Jakob discovered the law of large numbers. Johann solved the brachistochrone problem.

twist.text: They were fiercely jealous of each other. Johann once plagiarized Jakob's work after his death.

climax.text: Daniel Bernoulli, Johann's son, explained fluid dynamics—why airplane wings generate lift.

punchline.text: A family feud fueled three generations of genius.
